purpose: Machine-readable contract that binds infrastructure profiles to proxmox-k3s cluster configuration files without embedding local credentials.
resolutionRules: Provider-backed cycles resolve provider configuration through providerBindingId before invoking proxmox-k3s.
Local provider configuration is required for real create/delete/kubeconfig/template operations.
Example provider configuration is redacted and may be used for review, dry-run, or template derivation only.
Local provider configuration is the operational input for real provider execution; example and template files are review-safe inputs only.
Provider bindings must not contain Proxmox token secrets or kubeconfig credential material.
Provider bindings must expose the default lifecycle policy that will be applied when the cycle does not override it.
Create and delete command planning must use lifecycleCompatibility rather than inferring deletion from provider availability.
Delete commands require explicit confirmation even when an ephemeral lifecycle policy plans cluster teardown.
Provider bindings may declare templateVariables.template.vmidBase to render template.vmid_base in proxmox-k3s v1.0.3 cluster YAML files.
Provider bindings may declare vmidAllocationPolicy to document the intended VMID range and partial-provisioning cleanup behavior.
VMID allocation policy metadata must not be treated as proof of Proxmox state; real create operations remain authoritative through provider command results.
Real provider execution must resolve only localPath or an explicit safe override; examplePath and templatePath are review-only inputs.
Plan and dry-run workflows use review-safe provider configurations unless an explicit override is supplied.
When multiple worker VMs are materialized on the same Proxmox host, the binding should document that physical-host consolidation explicitly.
Provider bindings that rely on runtime-generated provisioning profiles must declare status=runtime_generated_per_variant and the provisioning template used for generation.
Operational provider invocations must use the proxmox-k3s v1.0.3 cluster command namespace: cluster create, cluster kubeconfig, and cluster delete.
Provider bindings may declare templateVariables.addons to document provider-managed add-ons used by proxmox-k3s v1.0.3 cluster YAML files.
